text:
There was an old woman named Olive who loved collecting old tales.

One cold evening she sat by an ancient oak tree.

And that tree spoke a hundred ears.

Olive listened eagerly

A hoot owl.

An echo.

A story--

Long ago there was a forest

Of talking creatures

And an old witch

With feathers as dark as a night storm

She was good-hearted

But very scary

She would visit the forest to feast

And take away every creature!

One day the birds decided

They would go forth and find the witch

Seek some wisdom

Some knowledge perhaps?

They flew westward

And flew past the edge

And there she was

A terrible woman

Who thought herself royalty?

The birds thought maybe

Maybe we'll listen...

They hoot-ed?

And Owl thought...

...

Just listen.

They hoot-ed harder

A story...

There was a holly bush

One prickly top

It heard the birds singing

That was naughty

It swo
